items.title: NT ranked last for illicit tobacco laws in new report

children.content: Smoking is one of the leading causes of death in the Northern Territory. Now, the jurisdiction has been ranked the weakest in the country for how it tackles cheap, illicit tobacco.

gallery.altText: A woman with shoulder-length blonde hair, inspecting the packets on cigarettes in her hands.

thumbnail.altText: A woman with shoulder-length blonde hair, inspecting the packets on cigarettes in her hands.

items.title: Communities 'in the middle of nowhere' are scared they'll run out of fuel

children.content: Remote communities are calling for assurance on fuel security in the face of potential shortages, along with financial assistance as already-high fuel and food prices continue to climb.

image.altText: Alastair King stands at the checkout register of a small grocery store, speaking to the worker behind the counter.

items.title: Al Kuwait ships cattle out of Darwin after weeks anchored near Fremantle

children.content: Anchored off Fremantle for weeks because the war in Iran had stopped the live sheep trade to the Middle East, the world's largest live export vessel has ventured north to Darwin.

image.altText: MV Al Kuwait at the Darwin Port

items.title: Hospital staff using ChatGPT to calculate medication doses, nurses claim

children.content: Whistleblowers say understaffing at the Northern Territory's busiest hospital is forcing nurses to take on unsafe patient loads, and some are teaching themselves medical procedures via YouTube.

gallery.altText: A nurse in uniform walking through the corridor of a hospital, facing away from the camera.

thumbnail.altText: A nurse in uniform walking through the corridor of a hospital, facing away from the camera.

items.title: Not all city suburbs have NBN access and it isn't by accident. Here's why

children.content: Despite popular belief, not all Australians are guaranteed access to the NBN — and as many consumers have discovered, alternative networks do not always provide quality service.

image.altText: A graphic showing a person's hands on a laptop with a Wifi failure symbol superimposed over the top.

avatar.alt: An Australian-Egyptian woman with dark brown shoulder-length curly hair smiling at camera, in a green patterned top.

items.title: NT councils flag cuts to street lights, pool hours as result of power prices

children.content: Power price hikes are expected to be passed onto local councils by the Northern Territory government in the coming month, with Darwin's lord mayor warning some community services could be cut as a result.

image.altText: A sign outside an old building reads: City of Darwin.

items.title: Flood-damaged roads cause 'catastrophic' impacts for NT businesses

children.content: Workers in road-reliant industries are calling on the federal government to provide urgent funding to repair the NT's road network, which was severely damaged by floods.

image.altText: A drone shot shows a damaged dirt road alongside a brown river, with a massive pothole missing from one section.

items.title: 'Derek' the dingo a concern for some as he roams streets in outback town

children.content: An lone dingo, affectionately known as Derek, has been spotted in suburban streets, backyards and even in dog parks, raising some concern for Alice Springs residents.

image.altText: A dingo walking on a street

items.title: Why Celine Cremer's family don't live with anger after remains found in forest

children.content: Backpacker Celine Cremer set off on a short walk with only a phone to guide her — and vanished. More than two years later, her family has an urgent message for other travellers.

items.title: The world is on the cusp of record hunger levels. Enter Vladimir Putin

children.content: With fears hunger could hit record levels, Vladimir Putin and Russia are poised to capitalise — financially and diplomatically.

image.altText: Vladimir Putin sits at a desk with a map in front of him in the Kremlin

items.title: Price of groceries to increase within weeks due to war in Iran

children.content: Farmers ask for a 30-cent-per-litre price rise for milk as fuel and fertiliser prices surge.

items.title: 'Hard to break the habit': Why free public transport isn't enough to shift from cars

children.content: Free public transport is a popular policy, but researchers say it's not enough to convince drivers to leave the car at home.

image.altText: A train on a city railway.

items.title: Two-thirds of Australians leave private information open to cybercriminals

children.content: New research suggests Australians are leaving themselves vulnerable to cybercrime through their public social media accounts.

image.altText: A pair of hands typing on a laptop

items.title: Blood donation eligibility expands for gay and bisexual men, transgender people

children.content: From today, Australian Red Cross Lifeblood will use gender-neutral assessments for blood and platelets donations.

items.title: The Queensland region where locals avoid their own hospitals

children.content: In Queensland's Lockyer Valley, new figures suggest a third of the region's 41 hospital beds are not regularly available for new patients.

items.title: US domestic violence shooting leaves eight children dead

children.content: The victims, who were shot at two homes in the US state of Louisiana, ranged in age from one to about 14 years old.

items.title: Who was C Gordon? NSW community tries to solve WWI soldier mystery

children.content: The names etched into the Araluen War Memorial are read aloud every Anzac Day, but one digger from World War I remains a mystery.

image.altText: Two women standing in front of a war memorial

items.title: 'I had no-one to talk to': The 92yo trying to save his dying language

children.content: Peter is the last living speaker of the First Nations language Thiinma. When he met linguist Rosie, they used music to help keep his knowledge alive, and formed a beautiful friendship along the way.

gallery.altText: Peter stands on stage at a microphone with bright lights behind him, wearing an akubra, shirt and bolo tie
Rosie holding a hat and laughing while Peter points at her, they are standing outside
A large stage lit up in orange and blue lights, with Peter on the microphone, Rosie at the piano, a cellist and Barton on yidaki

children.content: The two divers were spotted by a jet more than 14 nautical miles from where they went missing on Saturday morning.

items.title: Man faces Melbourne court after pedestrian death at comic convention

children.content: Awer Dau, 33, faces the Melbourne Magistrates' Court on Sunday evening, charged with culpable driving causing death. He allegedly hit a 20-year-old man while driving outside the Supanova Comic Con and Gaming convention on Saturday.

image.altText: Bunches of flowers against a fence.

items.title: Schools deemed safe after WA's biggest lithium-ion battery fire

children.content: Schools in Perth's south-east have been deemed safe for students to attend after WA's biggest lithium-ion battery fire sparked a HAZMAT warning in Maddington, which is still in place.

image.altText: Flames can be seen billowing from a building in an industrial area.

items.title: Tasmania's opposition has declared it's 'back', but the work isn't done yet

children.content: The Labor party's first state conference in seven years reunited old friends and potentially a few "frenemies", but with issues like greyhound racing absent, drama was largely kept at bay.

image.altText: A man in a suit stands in front of a red background

items.title: Netball star calls for pay increase after new broadcast deal

children.content: Diamonds and Melbourne Vixens star Jo Weston calls for a pay increase after a new free-to-air broadcast deal.

children.content: Even if Zac Lomax's starting debut for the Western Force had been disastrous, it would still deserve to be considered a success.

items.title: Demons cling on for epic win over Lions as Freo makes light work of Eagles

children.content: Melbourne digs deep to complete a thrilling two-point victory after a sensational tussle with Brisbane, while Fremantle claims Western Derby bragging rights with a 56-point humbling of West Coast.

items.title: Eels stun Bulldogs after having spirit and desire publicly questioned

children.content: One week after their coach publicly questioned their spirit and desire, Parramatta produces a boilover to beat the Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs.

items.title: Donald Trump might have annoyed the only ally who can help clear sea mines

children.content: The prospect of mines in the Strait of Hormuz has highlighted a gap in US naval capabilities and reinforced its need for NATO help.

items.title: Newcastle Jets crowned A-League premiers with help from arch rivals

children.content: Newcastle has claimed a maiden A-League Men's Premiers Plate after local rivals Central Coast stunned second-placed Auckland.

items.title: Eddie Nketia runs 100m faster than any Australian ever, but it doesn't count

children.content: Newly minted Australian sprinter Eddie Nketia breaks the national all-conditions 100m record set 23 years ago by Patrick Johnson.

image.altText: Eddie Nketia runs in a college sprint race ahead of another runner.

items.title: How these children turned a struggle with school into ambitious art show

children.content: At an art gallery in Sydney’s inner west, a group of mostly neurodivergent children are staging their first exhibition. For many of them, it is the first time they have felt seen.

gallery.altText: A boy with dark brown hair smiles with his thumbs up.

items.title: Couple says loophole allowing theft from cars shipped interstate

children.content: Elihu and Amanda Del Valle say thousands of dollars worth of their belongings were stolen from the boot of their car when it was shipped from Melbourne to Perth, but police aren't able to investigate.

image.altText: A close up photo of a man with dark hair, wearing a black top

items.title: Police investigate separate alleged murders near Newcastle

children.content: A man charged with an alleged domestic violence murder of a woman, believed to be his mother, is refused bail. Meanwhile, police are calling for public help after a man was stabbed by masked intruders at Argenton.

image.altText: two police vehicles out the front of a home
